pageCaption.value: Perpetual. For nearly a century, the word has been written on every Rolex Oyster watch. But Perpetual is more than a word on a dial. It is a philosophy - an unceasing quest for excellence, to explore and to share human knowledge to build a better world.

metadescription.value: For nearly a century, Rolex watches have accompanied expeditions to advance knowledge about the world. Today, Rolex supports scientists, conservationists and organizations to help safeguard the planet.

contentTitle.value: PERPETUAL IS MORE THAN A WORD ON A DIAL

contentText.value:
Perpetual. For nearly a century, the word has been written on every Rolex Oyster watch. But Perpetual is more than a word on a dial. It is a philosophy - an unceasing quest for excellence, to explore and to share human knowledge to build a better world.

It is based on a fundamental belief in unlimited human potential, in continuous improvement, in always pushing the boundaries and taking the long-term view. Our watches are built to last. So is our contribution to future generations.

text.value: Everything about Rolex carries the stamp of one exceptional human being: Hans Wilsdorf. The founder of Rolex was a visionary entrepreneur and an altruist, whose enduring philanthropy has left a mark that goes far beyond watchmaking.

pageCaption.value: Faced with a shortage of skilled watchmakers in the United States, Rolex built a school in 2001 to train a new generation of young specialists to service high-quality mechanical wristwatches.

metadescription.value: Faced with a shortage of skilled watchmakers in the United States, Rolex built a school in 2001 to train a new generation of young specialists to service high-quality mechanical wristwatches.

pageTitle.value: The man behind the crown - Hans Wilsdorf - Perpetual

pageCaption.value: Everything about Rolex carries the stamp of one exceptional human being. Hans Wilsdorf, the founder of Rolex, was not just a visionary entrepreneur whose stream of inventions, such as the first waterproof wristwatch, changed the world. He was an altruist, whose enduring philanthropy has left a mark that goes far beyond watchmaking.

pageTitle.value: Sir Francis Chichester - Time on the high seas

pageCaption.value: Few characterize the daring spirit that has marked the history of yachting better than Sir Francis Chichester, whose single-handed round-the-world voyage in 1966–1967 charted the course for subsequent generations of yachtsmen and yachtswomen.

WaPageTitle.value: Sir Francis Chichester - Time on the high seas

metadescription.value: Discover Sir Francis Chichester's incredible odyssey around the globe's oceans in record-breaking time and how he marked the history of yachting.

previewTitle.value: Sir Francis Chichester - Time on the high seas

pageCaption.value: In 1960, Jacques Piccard and Don Walsh piloted the Trieste on its plunge to the deepest point on Earth – a 10,916 metres (35,800-foot) depression called the challenger deep.

metadescription.value: In 1960, Jacques Piccard and Don Walsh piloted the Trieste on its plunge to the deepest point on Earth – a 10,916 metres (35,800-foot) depression called the challenger deep.

pageCaption.value: Perpetual. That is the word inscribed on every dial of an Oyster. Today it means so much more than a self-winding watch movement.

metadescription.value: Perpetual. That is the word inscribed on every dial of an Oyster. Today it means so much more than a self-winding watch movement.

pageCaption.value: The story of Rolex is intimately associated with human achievement. Hans Wilsdorf, the company’s founder, saw the mutual benefit of equipping people who were record breakers with an Oyster watch: the first of a long line of athletes and explorers was Mercedes Gleitze, the first British woman to swim the English Channel.

Hans Wilsdorf, the company’s founder, saw the mutual benefit of equipping people who were record breakers with an Oyster watch: the first of a long line of athletes and explorers was Mercedes Gleitze, the first British woman to swim the English Channel.

pageTitle.value: Making of Rolex Deepsea Challenge with James Cameron - Video

pageCaption.value: During his visit to Rolex Headquarters in Geneva, James Cameron discovers the making of the experimental Deepsea Challenge watch that accompanied him to the depths of the Mariana Trench. He shares with us his impressions of the watch and the skill of those who created it.

WaPageTitle.value: Making of Rolex Deepsea Challenge with James Cameron - Video

metadescription.value: During his visit to Rolex Headquarters in Geneva, James Cameron discovers the making of the experimental Deepsea Challenge watch that accompanied him to the depths of the Mariana Trench. He shares with us his impressions of the watch and the skill of those who created it.

previewTitle.value: Making of Rolex Deepsea Challenge with James Cameron - Video

pageCaption.value: When Rolex constructs or expands its headquarters and production facilities, it brings the same attention to aesthetics and detail as it gives to designing one of its prized chronometers.

metadescription.value: Read how Rolex's passion for unique, versatile and rational architecture, qualities shared by its timepieces, has always kept it in the forefront of innovation.

pageTitle.value: A stroke of genius in five letters - Perpetual

pageCaption.value: On 2 July 1908, Hans Wilsdorf officially filed and registered Rolex as a brand name in Switzerland – a master stroke that would shape the company’s future.

WaPageTitle.value: A stroke of genius in five letters - Perpetual

metadescription.value: On 2 July 1908, Hans Wilsdorf officially filed and registered Rolex as a brand name in Switzerland – a master stroke that would shape the company’s future.

previewTitle.value: A stroke of genius in five letters - Perpetual

pageTitle.value: Behind every great artist is a great artist - Perpetual

pageCaption.value: Rolex understands the art of mentoring. The skills and accomplishments of our watchmakers have been passed on from one generation to the next, ensuring the transmission of knowledge built up for more than a century. Similarly, an interest in contributing to the wider world, which was instilled by our founder Hans Wilsdorf, has led to a lasting contribution to the arts, part of the “Perpetual Spirit” that is his legacy and drives the company in its business and its many and varied interests.

WaPageTitle.value: Behind every great artist is a great artist - Perpetual

pageTitle.value: Rolex presents: The Trieste's Deepest Dive (Extended) - Video

pageCaption.value: In 1960, Lieutenant Don Walsh of the US Navy and Swiss oceanographer Jacques Piccard navigated the Trieste bathyscaphe into the Mariana Trench. They accomplished a feat so incredible that it forever raised the bar for deep-ocean exploration.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex presents: The Trieste's Deepest Dive (Extended) - Video

metadescription.value: In 1960, Lieutenant Don Walsh of the US Navy and Swiss oceanographer Jacques Piccard navigated the Trieste bathyscaphe into the Mariana Trench. They accomplished a feat so incredible that it forever raised the bar for deep-ocean exploration.

previewTitle.value: Rolex presents: The Trieste's Deepest Dive (Extended) - Video

pageTitle.value: Joining forces for the future - Environment

pageCaption.value: For many years Rolex has supported individuals and organizations whose aims have been to explore the Earth, find innovative solutions for positive change and raise awareness of critical environmental issues.

WaPageTitle.value: Joining forces for the future - Environment

metadescription.value: Discover how Rolex joins forces with geographical and marine societies, explorers, conservationists, geologists and glaciologists to improve the future.
